question.text: In the NSE derivatives segment, order matching follows which priority?

answer.text: Price priority first, then time priority for orders at the same price
Time priority first, regardless of price
Order size priority first, then price priority
Client orders have priority over proprietary orders at the same price

explanation.text:
**The Logic:** The NSE NEAT-F&O system follows price-time priority: (1) Best price gets first priority - for buy orders, highest price; for sell orders, lowest price. (2) Among orders at the same price, the order entered first (earliest timestamp) gets priority.
**The Trap:** There is no size priority or client vs proprietary priority in the matching algorithm. All orders are treated equally based solely on price and time.
**Cross-Exam Context:** Price-time priority is the standard order matching mechanism across Indian exchanges.
**Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Price-time priority: Best price matched first. Same price = earlier order matched first. No size or member-type preference.
